children.name: ParseCommand
children.purpose: Validate and parse the command string to determine the operation type.
children.behavior: Check that command is one of 'place', 'cancel', 'track'. If invalid, return error.
postconditions: parsed_command is one of 'place', 'cancel', 'track' or error is set

children.name: PlaceOrder
children.purpose: Execute the 'place' command: validate items, charge payment, reserve inventory, create order, send notification.
children.behavior: Sequentially call child functions for each step; if any fails, rollback previous steps.

semantic_inputs.name: order_data
semantic_inputs.description: Order payload with items, payment_method, shipping_address

semantic_outputs.name: order_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result with success, order_id, status, message

postconditions: If success, order created and payment charged; if fail, all changes rolled back

children.name: CancelOrder
children.purpose: Execute the 'cancel' command: verify order exists and not shipped, refund payment, restore inventory, update status.
children.behavior: Sequentially call child functions; rollback on failure.

semantic_outputs.name: order_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result with success, order_id, status, message

postconditions: If success, order cancelled and refunded; if fail, no changes

children.name: TrackOrder
children.purpose: Execute the 'track' command: retrieve order status and estimated delivery time.
children.behavior: Query orders database for order_id, return status and delivery estimate.

semantic_outputs.name: order_result
semantic_outputs.description: Result with success, order_id, status, message (includes delivery time)

postconditions: Order status and delivery time returned
children.stop_reason: Simple query but may involve multiple data sources

decomposition_rationale: The parent OrderSystem function is decomposed into a command parser and three command-specific executors. ParseCommand validates the command, then the parent routes to PlaceOrder, CancelOrder, or TrackOrder based on the parsed command. Each executor handles its own atomic workflow, ensuring separation of concerns and single responsibility.
